title: DNA extraction from concrete
author: Anders Kiledal
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b2i5qcg6
materials: Quantify with Qubit and store at -20°C.

steps.title: Wash sample holder and puck of...
steps.instruction: Wash sample holder and puck of the ring and puck mill with soap and hot water. Rinse thoroughly and dry.

steps.title: Rinse sample holder and puck 2x with 95% ethanol
steps.instruction: Rinse sample holder and puck 2x with 95% ethanol.

steps.title: Sterilize sample holder and puck under...
steps.instruction: Sterilize sample holder and puck under germicidal UV light for 40 min.

steps.title: Roughly break ~10 g concrete with...
steps.instruction: Roughly break ~10 g concrete with a hammer into pieces that are small enough to fit into a ring and puck mill or equivalent grinder

steps.title: Grind samples to powder (usually requires 1-5 minutes of grinding)
steps.instruction: Grind samples to powder (usually requires 1-5 minutes of grinding). The ground powder can be stored at -20°C (or -80°C) prior to DNA extraction.

steps.title: In a 50 mL conical tube,...
steps.instruction: In a 50 mL conical tube, place 10 g pulverized concrete, 5 mL 0.5 M EDTA, 150 µL Proteinase K (20 mg/mL), 138 µL 20% SDS, and 0.2 mL glacial acetic acid. Incubate at 55°C overnight with gentle rotation.

steps.title: Some samples may absorb a large...
steps.instruction: Some samples may absorb a large volume of the pre-treatment solution, leaving very little supernatant. If this happens, add 5-10 mL of sterile water before vortexing.

steps.title: Vortex at maximum speed on a...
steps.instruction: Vortex at maximum speed on a platform vortexer for 10 min (this is similar to bead beating, but using the material itself instead of beads).

steps.title: Centrifuge 3 min, 5000 RPM
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3 min, 5000 RPM

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to new 50 mL conical tube
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to new 50 mL conical tube

steps.title: Add 30 mL QGmod
steps.instruction: Add 30 mL QGmod. If solution is purple (this indicates that the pH > 7.5), adjust pH with acetic acid until solution is yellow (neutral or acidic). Add 125 µl silica suspension (well mixed) and 5 µl yeast RNA (1 mg/mL, acts as a carrier molecule). Mix well and allow to bind overnight at room temperature with gentle rotation.

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 min at 5000 RPM to pellet silica
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 min at 5000 RPM to pellet silica.

steps.title: Decant supernatant (pour off, centrifuge the...
steps.instruction: Decant supernatant (pour off, centrifuge the tube briefly, and carefully pipette off what remains to prevent carryover of Buffer QG. Discard supernatant.)

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 10 mL 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 10 mL 80% EtOH. (To prevent clogging the pipet tips, wait ~5 min after adding ethanol before trying to resuspend. It may be necessary to scrape the tube over a tube rack or bang it on the bench to resuspend the silica particles. The solution can also be stirred with the tip. Try to break up any large clumps before attempting to draw the solution into the pipet tip.)

steps.title: Centrifuge 30 min, 5000 RPM, 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 30 min, 5000 RPM, 4°C

steps.title: Decant supernatant (pour off, pulse in...
steps.instruction: Decant supernatant (pour off, pulse in centrifuge, carefully pipette off what remains)

steps.title: Resuspend silica pellet in 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend silica pellet in 1 mL 80% ethanol and transfer solution to 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube

steps.title: Centrifuge 3 min at 13000 RPM
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3 min at 13000 RPM

steps.title: Decant or pipette off supernatant
steps.instruction: Decant or pipette off supernatant

steps.title: Air dry silica with bound DNA
steps.instruction: Air dry silica with bound DNA. This usually takes ~1 hour at room temperature in a PCR hood.

steps.title: Resuspend in 50 µL 10 mM...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 50 µL 10 mM Tris, pre-warmed to 60°C, then elute for 5 min at 60°C with gentle rotation. Centrifuge 3 min at 13000 RPM and transfer supernatant (with DNA) to fresh tube. Repeat and combine eluates.

steps.title: A minuscule amount of silica may...
steps.instruction: A minuscule amount of silica may remain but should not interfere with downstream applications. Any remaining silica can be pelleted to reduce future transfer.
